project.name: CodeFab
project.description: A Python-based interpreter/compiler pipeline for a custom language with an interactive REPL shell. Built by team Ctrl-C.

nodes.name: assembler.py
nodes.summary: Implements the recursive-descent parser (Assembler) for the CodeFab custom language, converting a flat token stream into a structured AST of Stmt and Expr nodes. Handles all statement types (var declaration, print, if/else, for, block) and expression types (assignment, logical, comparison, binary, unary, grouping, primary).

nodes.name: Assembler
nodes.summary: Recursive-descent parser that transforms a list of Token objects into a list of Stmt nodes representing the program's abstract syntax tree. Implements Pratt-style precedence climbing through methods like logic_or, logic_and, equality, comparison, term, factor, and unary.

nodes.name: test_assembler.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive pytest test suite for the Assembler parser, verifying correct AST construction for all statement and expression forms including number literals, binary expressions, operator precedence, grouping, unary operators, strings, booleans, variable declarations, assignments, comparisons, logical expressions, block statements, if/else, and for loops.

nodes.name: expr.py
nodes.summary: Defines the abstract syntax tree (AST) expression node hierarchy for the CodeFab language, providing data classes for literals, variables, assignments, binary/unary/grouping/logical expressions.
nodes.languageNotes: All eight expression types inherit from the base Expr class using a simple Python dataclass-like pattern with __init__ and __repr__ methods.

nodes.name: Expr
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for all expression AST nodes in the CodeFab language.

nodes.name: LiteralExpr
nodes.summary: AST node representing a literal value (number, string, boolean, or null).

nodes.name: VariableExpr
nodes.summary: AST node representing a variable reference, holding the identifier token.

nodes.name: AssignExpr
nodes.summary: AST node representing a variable assignment expression, holding the target name token and the value expression.

nodes.name: BinaryExpr
nodes.summary: AST node for a binary operation (arithmetic or comparison) with left operand, operator token, and right operand.

nodes.name: UnaryExpr
nodes.summary: AST node for a unary operation (e.g., negation or logical not) with operator token and operand expression.

nodes.name: GroupingExpr
nodes.summary: AST node for a parenthesized grouping expression, wrapping an inner expression for precedence control.

nodes.name: LogicalExpr
nodes.summary: AST node for a logical (and/or) expression with left, operator, and right sub-expressions.

nodes.name: statement.py
nodes.summary: Defines the AST statement node hierarchy for the CodeFab language, providing data classes for expression statements, print, variable declarations, blocks, if/else, and for loops.
nodes.languageNotes: Statement nodes parallel the expression hierarchy and are consumed by the executor for interpretation.

nodes.name: Stmt
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for all statement AST nodes in the CodeFab language.

nodes.name: ExpressionStmt
nodes.summary: AST node wrapping an expression as a standalone statement.

nodes.name: PrintStmt
nodes.summary: AST node representing a print statement that outputs the value of its expression.

nodes.name: VarStmt
nodes.summary: AST node for a variable declaration statement with name token and optional initializer expression.

nodes.name: BlockStmt
nodes.summary: AST node for a block of statements enclosed in braces, creating a new scope.

nodes.name: IfStmt
nodes.summary: AST node for an if/else conditional statement with condition expression, then branch, and optional else branch.

nodes.name: ForStmt
nodes.summary: AST node for a for loop statement with initializer, condition, increment, and body sub-components.

nodes.name: test_assembler_statement.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests verifying that each statement AST node class correctly stores its constituent fields (name, initializer, condition, branches, body).

nodes.name: checker.py
nodes.summary: Facade class that wires together the scope stack, error reporter, and expression/statement resolvers into a single `check()` entry point for the semantic analysis phase.

nodes.name: Checker
nodes.summary: Orchestrates semantic checking by composing ScopeStack, ErrorReporter, ExpressionResolver, and StatementResolver; exposes a single `check(statements)` method that resets shared state and performs a full resolution pass.

nodes.name: error_reporter.py
nodes.summary: Provides a simple stateful error accumulator used by the checker to collect and retrieve semantic errors encountered during name resolution.

nodes.name: ErrorReporter
nodes.summary: Accumulates error messages emitted during semantic analysis; supports reset between runs and exposes the collected errors as a list.

nodes.name: resolver.py
nodes.summary: Implements the semantic resolution pass with two visitor-style classes — ExpressionResolver and StatementResolver — that walk the AST to detect uninitialized reads, self-references, and duplicate declarations, with flow-sensitive snapshot/merge logic for if/for control flow.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a dispatch-table pattern (dict keyed by AST node type) instead of the traditional Visitor pattern, avoiding isinstance chains in favor of O(1) method lookup.

nodes.name: ExpressionResolver
nodes.summary: Walks expression AST nodes to detect uninitialized variable reads and self-references in initializers, dispatching to per-node-type handlers for Variable, Assign, Binary, Unary, Grouping, and Literal expressions.

nodes.name: StatementResolver
nodes.summary: Walks statement AST nodes to enforce declaration uniqueness within scopes, managing scope lifecycle and performing flow-sensitive snapshot/restore/merge to reason about variable initialization across if-branches and for-loops.

nodes.name: scope_stack.py
nodes.summary: Implements a lexical scope stack that tracks variable declaration and initialization state, supporting scope creation, declaration, initialization, snapshot/restore for flow analysis, and reverse-order scope lookup.

nodes.name: ScopeStack
nodes.summary: A stack of scope dictionaries tracking variable initialization states; provides declare/initialize/mark_initialized/is_uninitialized operations plus snapshot/restore for flow-sensitive analysis across branches.

nodes.name: test_checker.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive pytest test suite for the checker module, organized into six test classes covering duplicate declaration, self-reference in initializers, uninitialized variable access, control-flow traversal, deep nested structures, and flow-sensitive branch/loop semantics.

nodes.name: TestDuplicateDeclaration
nodes.summary: Tests that duplicate variable declarations are reported as errors in global and block scopes, while legal shadowing in nested or sibling blocks is correctly permitted.

nodes.name: TestSelfReferenceInInitializer
nodes.summary: Tests detection of variables whose initializer expression references the variable being declared, both directly and nested inside expression trees or inner blocks.

nodes.name: TestUninitializedVariableAccess
nodes.summary: Tests that reading a declared-but-not-yet-initialized variable is an error, reading an initialized one is allowed, and that assignment properly transitions a variable to initialized.

nodes.name: TestDfsTraversalOverControlFlow
nodes.summary: Tests that semantic errors nested inside if-branches, else-branches, and for-loop initializers are all discovered during a DFS traversal of the AST, including multi-error scenarios.

nodes.name: TestComplexNestedTraversal
nodes.summary: Tests deeply nested and complex AST structures — four-level block nesting, shadow chains, errors buried inside compound expressions, and multi-branch error aggregation — verifying that sibling subtrees do not contaminate each other's scope state.

nodes.name: TestStmtFlowSensitivity
nodes.summary: Tests flow-sensitive initialization tracking: verifies that a variable assigned only in the then-branch is still uninitialized in the else-branch, that loop bodies that may not execute do not mark variables as initialized, and that an increment in the loop increment expression cannot hide an uninitialized read in the first iteration.

nodes.name: gist-acceptance.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that runs the Gist acceptance test suite on every pull request, push to main, and manual dispatch, using Python 3.13 and pytest.

nodes.name: Run gist acceptance tests
nodes.summary: Executes the gist acceptance test file using pytest with PYTHONPATH set to the project root.

nodes.name: lint.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that enforces code style on every pull request and push to main, running black, isort, and ruff checks against the full codebase using Python 3.13.

nodes.name: Check formatting
nodes.summary: Verifies that all Python source files conform to black formatting rules.

nodes.name: Check import order
nodes.summary: Verifies import statement ordering across all Python files using isort.

nodes.name: Run ruff
nodes.summary: Runs ruff linter to catch style and correctness issues across the entire codebase.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project entry-point document for the CodeFab (Ctrl-C team) repository, covering team ground rules, PR/code-review workflow requiring two approvals, CI/lint setup using black, isort, and ruff, branch naming conventions, and PR label criteria.

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Minimal pyproject.toml configuring isort to use the black-compatible profile, ensuring consistent import ordering across the project's Python source files.

nodes.name: requirements-dev.txt
nodes.summary: Development dependency manifest pinning the linting tools (black, isort, ruff) and testing stack (pytest, pytest-cov, pytest-mock) required for local development and CI runs.

nodes.name: tokenizer.py
nodes.summary: Defines the lexical analysis stage of the pipeline: a TokenType enum of all recognized token categories, a Token value type, and a Tokenizer class that converts raw source strings into a flat list of Token objects.
nodes.languageNotes: TokenType is implemented as a plain Python class (not enum.Enum); token equality uses getattr-based duck typing to allow flexible comparisons in tests.

nodes.name: TokenType
nodes.summary: Enumerates all token categories recognized by the custom language, including operators, literals, keywords (if, for, var, print), and control tokens (EOF, SEMICOLON).

nodes.name: Token
nodes.summary: Immutable value object representing a single lexical token, carrying its TokenType, the original source string, and an optional literal value.

nodes.name: Tokenizer
nodes.summary: Stateful lexer that walks a source string character by character, dispatching to specialized readers for strings, numbers, identifiers, and single/multi-character operators, and emits a list of Token objects.

nodes.name: executor.py
nodes.summary: Implements runtime evaluation of the custom language's AST: the Environment class provides scoped variable storage with lexical chaining, and the Executor class walks statement and expression nodes to produce output and raise typed runtime errors.
nodes.languageNotes: Environment implements enclosing-scope chaining by keeping a reference to a parent Environment, enabling block-scoped shadowing with no separate symbol table.

nodes.name: Environment
nodes.summary: Lexically scoped variable store that chains to an enclosing Environment for lookup and assignment, raising CodeFabRuntimeError for undefined or undeclared variables.

nodes.name: Executor
nodes.summary: Tree-walking interpreter that dispatches over all Stmt and Expr node types to evaluate expressions, execute statements (print, var, if, for, block), and collect string output, with runtime type and operand validation.

nodes.name: test_assembler_expr.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests verifying that all AST expression node types (LiteralExpr, VariableExpr, AssignExpr, BinaryExpr, UnaryExpr, GroupingExpr, LogicalExpr) are constructed correctly and retain the expected fields and types.

nodes.name: test_assembler_tokenizer.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive unit test suite for the Tokenizer, covering number literals (integers, decimals, zero), arithmetic and comparison operators, keywords (if, else, var, for, true, false, and, or, print), strings, block/grouping delimiters, and error cases for malformed input.

nodes.name: test_codefab_gist_acceptance.py
nodes.summary: End-to-end acceptance test suite that drives the full pipeline (Tokenizer → Assembler → Checker → Executor) from source strings, verifying output values and expected runtime error messages across a range of language features.

nodes.name: run_source
nodes.summary: Helper that orchestrates the full pipeline for a source string: tokenizes, parses, checks, and executes it, returning the collected output lines as a list of strings.

nodes.name: test_executor.py
nodes.summary: Unit test suite for the Executor covering arithmetic and comparison binary expressions, operator precedence, grouping, unary negation, variable declaration and assignment, block scoping, if/else branching, logical and/or, for loops, and runtime error conditions (undefined variable, divide-by-zero, type mismatch).

nodes.name: pull_request_template.md
nodes.summary: GitHub pull request template enforcing a structured self-review checklist covering change description, reason, test steps, and ground rules compliance.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Gitignore-style configuration file listing patterns for files and directories to exclude from understand-anything analysis; all exclusion patterns are commented out by default.

nodes.name: main.py
nodes.summary: Top-level entry point for the CodeFab interpreter: instantiates a PromptShell and starts the interactive REPL loop.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Bootstraps the application by creating a PromptShell instance and calling its run() method to start the interactive shell.

nodes.name: shell.py
nodes.summary: Implements the interactive REPL shell for the CodeFab language, wiring together the tokenizer, assembler, checker, and executor into a user-facing prompt loop with command history and recommendation features.
nodes.languageNotes: The shell routes built-in commands (exit, ctrlc, ctrlv) before dispatching to the pipeline, and maintains a bounded history list used for frequency-based command recommendation.

nodes.name: PromptShell
nodes.summary: The central REPL class that accepts user input, routes built-in shell commands, runs code through the full pipeline (tokenize → parse → check → execute), and manages a bounded command history for frequency-based recommendation.

nodes.name: test_shell.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive unit tests for PromptShell covering code execution, history management, bounded history size, ctrlc/ctrlv recommendation flow, exit handling, and empty input edge cases.

layers.name: Lexer
layers.description: Converts raw source text into typed Token objects that feed the parser.

layers.name: AST Data Model
layers.description: Pure data classes defining the expression and statement node types that make up the abstract syntax tree.

layers.name: Parser
layers.description: Recursive-descent parser that consumes the token stream and produces a typed AST.

layers.name: Semantic Analysis
layers.description: Flow-sensitive checker that detects scope errors, uninitialized reads, duplicate declarations, and self-references before execution.

layers.name: Runtime
layers.description: Tree-walking interpreter that evaluates the validated AST against a scoped environment to produce program output.

layers.name: Shell
layers.description: Interactive REPL entry point that orchestrates the full pipeline — tokenize, parse, check, execute — for each user-submitted line, with command history and recall.

layers.name: Test Suite
layers.description: Unit, integration, and end-to-end acceptance tests covering every pipeline stage from tokenizer through shell.

layers.name: Project Support
layers.description: CI workflows, project documentation, code-style configuration, and tooling ignore rules that govern contribution and quality gates.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: README.md is the front door to CodeFab: it explains the project's purpose, defines team ground rules, and describes the PR workflow and CI/lint setup every contributor must follow. Reading it first gives you the vocabulary and working conventions that the rest of the tour assumes. It is the single document a newcomer should read before touching any code.

tour.title: Tokenizer — Lexical Analysis
tour.description: The tokenizer is the first stage of the pipeline: it reads a raw source string and produces a flat sequence of typed Token objects. Every subsequent stage — the parser, checker, and executor — depends on the output of this module, which explains why it has the highest fan-in score in the graph. Understanding what a Token is and how the lexer categorizes characters is the foundation for everything that follows.
tour.languageLesson: Python lexers commonly use a single-character lookahead loop with match/case or chained if/elif blocks. Representing token types as an Enum makes downstream pattern-matching safe and IDE-friendly.

tour.title: AST Node Definitions
tour.description: Before the parser can build a tree, you need the tree's node types. expr.py and statement.py define the full Abstract Syntax Tree hierarchy: expression nodes (literals, binary ops, identifiers) and statement nodes (assignments, control flow, function definitions). These data classes are the shared vocabulary between the parser, checker, and executor — all three import them directly, giving them the joint-highest fan-in in the project.
tour.languageLesson: Python dataclasses or named tuples are a lightweight way to define AST nodes without boilerplate. Using a base class (Expr / Stmt) and subclassing per node type enables isinstance checks and the Visitor pattern in downstream passes.

tour.title: Parser — Token Stream to AST
tour.description: The Assembler is CodeFab's recursive-descent parser: it consumes the token stream produced by the tokenizer and constructs a tree of Expr and Statement nodes. Each grammar rule corresponds to a method on the parser class, and the method hierarchy mirrors the language's operator precedence. This is the bridge between the text representation of a program and its structured, in-memory form.
tour.languageLesson: Recursive-descent parsers encode grammar rules directly as mutually recursive methods. Operator precedence is handled by calling higher-precedence rules from inside lower-precedence ones — addition calls multiplication, which calls unary, which calls primary.

tour.title: Checker — Semantic Error Reporting
tour.description: error_reporter.py is a stateful accumulator that collects semantic errors found during analysis. Rather than raising exceptions immediately, the checker gathers all problems so that a single pass can report multiple issues to the user at once. This design separates the concern of 'finding errors' from 'what to do about them', making the checker easier to test and extend.

tour.title: Checker — Scope Stack
tour.description: scope_stack.py implements a lexical scope stack that tracks which variables are declared and initialized at each level of nesting. The snapshot/restore capability is used during flow analysis to speculatively enter branches (like if/else bodies) and then roll back scope state afterward. This is the data structure that makes the resolver's control-flow analysis possible.
tour.languageLesson: A scope stack is a list of dictionaries, where each dictionary represents one lexical scope. Entering a block pushes a new dict; leaving pops it. Variable lookup walks the stack from top to bottom, implementing shadowing naturally.

tour.title: Checker — Resolver
tour.description: resolver.py is the main semantic analysis pass: it walks the AST produced by the parser, uses the scope stack to track declarations, and uses the error reporter to flag uninitialized reads and duplicate declarations. Because it imports both AST node types, both checker support modules, and the error reporter, it has the highest fan-out of any file in the project — it ties everything in the checker layer together.
tour.languageLesson: The Visitor pattern is common in AST passes: define a visit_NodeType method for each AST node class, then dispatch by node type. Python can implement this with getattr(self, f'visit_{type(node).__name__}', self.visit_generic)(node).

tour.title: Checker Facade
tour.description: checker.py is the public-facing entry point for the entire semantic analysis layer. It exposes a single check() function that constructs the resolver and error reporter, runs the analysis, and returns results. Downstream consumers — the shell and acceptance tests — call only this facade, never the internal resolver or scope stack directly. This encapsulation means the checker's internals can change without affecting callers.

tour.title: Executor — Tree-Walking Interpreter
tour.description: executor.py is the final stage of the pipeline: it walks the validated AST and evaluates it, maintaining a scoped environment that maps variable names to runtime values. It handles assignment, arithmetic, control flow, and function calls by recursively visiting AST nodes. After the checker has verified the program is semantically sound, the executor runs it — making this the module where source code becomes observable behavior.
tour.languageLesson: A tree-walking interpreter carries an 'environment' (a dict or chain of dicts) alongside the AST. Scoped environments mirror the scope stack from the checker — entering a block creates a child environment; exiting restores the parent. This symmetry makes it easier to reason about correctness.

tour.title: Interactive REPL Shell
tour.description: shell.py implements PromptShell, the interactive Read-Eval-Print Loop that wires all four pipeline stages together. For each line the user types, PromptShell runs the tokenizer, then the parser, then the checker, then the executor — in sequence — and prints the result or any errors. It also manages command history (scrollable with arrow keys), Ctrl+C recommendation when input looks erroneous, and Ctrl+V recall to re-run the previous command. This is the runtime home of the entire CodeFab pipeline.

tour.title: Test Suite — Unit Tests per Layer
tour.description: Each pipeline stage has a dedicated test file: test_assembler_tokenizer.py for the lexer, test_assembler_expr.py and test_assembler_statement.py for AST node types, test_assembler.py for the parser, test_checker.py for semantic analysis, test_executor.py for the interpreter, and test_shell.py for the REPL behavior (history, Ctrl+C/V, exit, command filtering). Keeping tests co-located by layer makes it straightforward to find what is covered and where a regression originated.

tour.title: End-to-End Acceptance Tests
tour.description: test_codefab_gist_acceptance.py exercises the full pipeline end-to-end by running complete programs from source string through tokenizer, parser, checker, and executor, asserting on final output or error messages. These tests catch integration bugs that unit tests miss — a token change that breaks the checker, or a scope rule that corrupts executor output. They are the highest-confidence signal that the pipeline works as a whole.

tour.title: CI Pipelines
tour.description: Two GitHub Actions workflows keep the codebase healthy automatically. lint.yml enforces formatting (black, isort) and static analysis (ruff) on every push, catching style drift before it reaches review. gist-acceptance.yml runs the acceptance test suite to ensure the full pipeline works end-to-end. Together they form the automated quality gate that all PRs must pass, reflecting the team ground rules described in the README.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions workflows use 'on' triggers (push, pull_request) to start jobs. A job is a sequence of steps that run on a fresh runner. Separating lint and test into two workflows allows them to run in parallel and lets reviewers pinpoint which concern failed.

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: main.py is where everything comes together: it instantiates PromptShell from shell.py and calls its start method, launching the interactive REPL. This three-line file is intentionally minimal — it delegates all logic to the shell, which in turn delegates to the four pipeline stages. Reading main.py last, after understanding each stage individually, reveals how CodeFab's architecture flows from a single entry point through a clean, linear pipeline to produce an interactive language interpreter.
